text.title: reranking-diversity-mmr Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for reranking-diversity-mmr. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: MMR requires document embeddings at rerank time, not just relevance scores. If the pipeline discards embeddings after indexing (to save memory), embeddings must be recomputed for the candidate pool before MMR can run. This doubles embedding cost for the candidate pool.
rule.rationale: MMR uses cosine similarity between candidate embeddings and already-selected document embeddings to compute the diversity term. Without embeddings, only the relevance term is available, which reduces MMR to standard reranking.

rule.statement: Use MMR when top-k results from standard reranking are near-duplicates. A simple heuristic: if cosine similarity between the top-2 results exceeds 0.92, the corpus has a redundancy problem that MMR should address.
rule.rationale: Near-duplicate passages (similarity > 0.92) waste context window tokens without adding new information. MMR resolves this by penalizing documents too similar to already-selected ones.

rule.statement: When evaluating MMR quality, measure both relevance (e.g., NDCG of the MMR result set vs. ground truth) and diversity (average pairwise similarity between selected documents). A good MMR configuration reduces average pairwise similarity by 20-40% with less than 5% NDCG drop.
rule.rationale: Optimizing MMR lambda without measuring the relevance/diversity tradeoff is tuning blind. The 20-40% diversity gain target ensures meaningful redundancy reduction while the 5% NDCG tolerance prevents sacrificing answer quality.
